yes | father = [[Hadj Boudebza]] | mother = | spouse = | education = | alma_mater = | relatives = }} }} '''John Boudebza''' (born 6 June 1990) is a French professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for [[Palau Broncos]] and previously [[Lézignan Sangliers]] in the [[Elite One Championship]], [[London Broncos]] in the [[Kingstone Press Championship]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] and at international level for [[France national rugby league team|France]]. His position is {{rlp|hk}}. ==Club career== ===Early career=== Boudebza played in the [[Elite One Championship]] for [[SM Pia XIII]] and [[Lézignan Sangliers]]. His father, [[Hadj Boudebza]] is a former rugby league player who played for [[Paris Saint-Germain Rugby League|Paris Saint-Germain]], [[AS Saint-Estève]] and for [[France national rugby league team|France]], disputing the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]]. ===Hull KR=== Boudebza joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on a one-year deal prior to the [[Super League XX|2015 Super League season]].[http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Hull-KR-confirm-capture-John-Boudebza-Lezignan/story-23305016-detail/story.html Hull KR confirm capture of John Boudebza from Lezignan] He appeared in the [[2015 Challenge Cup]] Final for Hull KR. Boudebza left Hull KR following their relegation from Super League in 2016. ===FC Lézignan XIII=== On 30 Nov 2016 it was reported that he had signed for [[FC Lézignan XIII]] in the [[Elite One Championship]]{{cite web|website=Total Rugby League|date=30 Nov 2016|accessdate=11 Jan 2021|title=John Boudebza finds himself a new club with Lezignan return|url=https://www.totalrl.com/john-boudebza-finds-new-club-lezignan-return/}} ===London Broncos=== After taking time off to heal long-standing injuries following his departure from Hull KR, Boudebza joined the [[London Broncos]] in July 2017.[http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/former-hull-kr-hooker-john-165078 Former Hull KR hooker John Boudebza joins London Broncos until the end of the season] ==International career== Boudebza made his international début for [[France national rugby league team|France]] in a victory over [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] in the [[2014 European Cup (rugby league)|2014 European Cup]]. He scored his first international point with a field goal against [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] in the [[2015 European Cup]] competition. He also played for France in their 2015 European Cup mid-tournament test-match against England. He was a part what was considered a 'weakened' French side due to injury and it showed with an appalling showing against their opponents.{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live/match/43791/report|title=England demolish France 84-4 in record win|publisher=Skysports.com|accessdate=24 October 2015}} ==References== {{reflist|30em}} ==External links== *[https://londonbroncosrl.com/players/john-boudezba/ London Broncos profile] *[http://www.ffr13.fr/wp-content/uploads/2015/08/boudebza-john-791x1024.jpg France profile] *[http://www.rlwc2017.com/players?team=380#John-Boudebza 2017 RLWC profile] {{France 2017 Rugby League World Cup squad}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Boudebza, John}} [[Category:1990 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:21st-century French sportsmen]] [[Category:AS Saint Estève players]] [[Category:Baroudeurs de Pia XIII players]] [[Category:French rugby league players]] [[Category:France national rugby league team players]] [[Category:Hull Kingston Rovers players]] [[Category:Lézignan Sangliers players]] [[Category:London Broncos players]] [[Category:Palau XIII Broncos players]] [[Category:Rugby league hookers]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Perpignan]] {{France-rugbyleague-bio-stub}}
